sections.paragraph:
δίαιτα, ης, ἡ (cp. διαιτάομαι ‘lead one’s life, live’; Pind., Hdt. et al.; LXX; TestSol 11:2 P; TestJos 3:5 [v.l. τροφήν]; Jos., Bell. 2, 151, C. Ap. 2, 240) orig. ‘way of life’.

① food, diet Dg 5:4.

② habitation, dwelling-place (Diod S 3, 30, 2; Ps.-Aristot., De Mundo 6, 398b, 32; Plut., Mor. 515e; OGI 383, 27 [I b.c.]; Jos., Ant. 15, 331; Tat. 9, 1 τῆς ἐν οὐρανῷ διαίτης life in heaven. So as loanw. in rabb.) 1 Cl 39:8 (Job 5:3). δ. τῆς σκηνῆς σου οὐ μὴ ἁμάρτῃ the dwelling-place of your tent shall not suffer want 56:13 (Job 5:24).—DELG.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

orig. orig. = original(ly)

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Ps.-Aristot Ps.-Aristot , s. Aristot.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 229
